ddls:
CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "iso_code" TEXT /* ISO code of the country */,
  "population" TEXT /* Population of the country */,
  "area_km2" REAL /* Area of the country in square kilometers */,
  "capital" TEXT /* Capital city of the country */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region or continent where the country is located */,
  "currency" TEXT /* Official currency of the country */,
  "government_type" TEXT /* Type of government (e.g., democracy, monarchy) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "economic_indicators" (
  "indicator_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each economic indicator record */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country associated with the indicator */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the economic data */,
  "gdp_per_capita" REAL /* GDP per capita value for the specified country and year */,
  "inflation_rate" REAL /* Inflation rate for the specified year */,
  "unemployment_rate" REAL /* Unemployment rate for the specified year */,
  "debt_to_gdp" REAL /* Debt-to-GDP ratio for the specified year */,
  "fiscal_deficit" REAL /* Fiscal deficit for the specified year */,
  "trade_balance" REAL /* Trade balance for the specified year */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data source for this indicator */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("indicator_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_economic_indicators_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_economic_indicators_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "years" (
  "year_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each year */,
  "year" INTEGER /* The year itself (e.g., 2002, 2003) */,
  "is_leap_year" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the year is a leap year */,
  "global_events" TEXT /* Significant global events that occurred in the year (e.g., financial crises, pandemics) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("year_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "gdp_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each GDP category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the GDP category (e.g., Low, Medium, High) */,
  "min_gdp" REAL /* Minimum GDP per capita for the category */,
  "max_gdp" REAL /* Maximum GDP per capita for the category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the GDP category */,
  "color_code" TEXT /* Color code for visual representation in reports and dashboards */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source (e.g., World Bank) */,
  "source_type" TEXT /* Type of data source (e.g., government, NGO) */,
  "source_url" TEXT /* URL of the data source */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date when the data source was last updated */,
  "reliability_score" INTEGER /* Reliability score of the data source (e.g., on a scale of 1 to 10) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the report was created */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the report */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the report */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the report (e.g., draft, published, archived) */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* Path to the report file (if stored externally) */,
  "shared_with" TEXT /* List of user IDs the report is shared with */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., analyst, researcher, admin) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user account (e.g., active, suspended) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the data was accessed */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Type of data accessed (e.g., report, raw data) */,
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the report accessed (if applicable) */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the specific data accessed (if applicable) */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit, download) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_logs_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES economic_indicators ("indicator_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_logs_report_id FOREIGN KEY ("report_id") REFERENCES reports ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "gdp_growth_rates" (
  "growth_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each growth rate record */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country associated with the growth rate */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the growth rate calculation */,
  "growth_rate" REAL /* Calculated growth rate for the specified country and year */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data source for this growth rate */,
  "methodology" TEXT /* Methodology used to calculate the growth rate */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("growth_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_gdp_growth_rates_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_gdp_growth_rates_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "country_categories" (
  "country_category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country category relationship */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country being categorized */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the GDP category associated with the country */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year when the country was categorized */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data source for this categorization */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_country_categories_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_country_categories_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_country_categories_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES gdp_categories ("category_id")
)
